task_completion.description: Navigate to the Artifactory web interface
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser is open with the URL bar showing 'http://localhost:8082' and the JFrog Artifactory login page or dashboard is visible.

task_completion.description: Log in to Artifactory using admin credentials
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Artifactory interface shows that the user is logged in, typically indicated by 'admin' or an admin profile icon in the top right corner.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Administration or Settings module
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Administration section is active, usually indicated by a highlighted gear icon in the sidebar or an 'Administration' header.

task_completion.description: Open the MIME Types configuration page
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'MIME Types' settings page is visible in the main content area, showing a list of existing file extensions and their corresponding MIME types.

task_completion.description: Add and save the custom MIME type for the 'datalog' extension
task_completion.visual_evidence: The MIME types list or a filled-out configuration form clearly shows the Extension 'datalog' mapped to the MIME Type 'application/vnd.company.datalog'.

integrity.description: The agent used a web browser to configure the application rather than terminal commands
integrity.visual_evidence: A web browser window (like Chrome or Firefox) is visible in the screenshots, and there are no terminal windows showing 'curl' or API scripts being used to bypass the GUI.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the genuine JFrog Artifactory interface
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the distinct JFrog Artifactory web UI components (sidebar, headers, specific styling) rather than a generic text editor or a mock webpage.

integrity.description: The agent actually saved the configuration rather than just typing it in without submitting
integrity.visual_evidence: The final screenshot shows the new MIME type present in the saved list of MIME types, or a success toast/notification is visible, rather than just showing text typed into an unsubmitted 'New MIME Type' modal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target MIME type to be configured is 'application/vnd.company.datalog'. The target file extension is 'datalog'. Artifactory is hosted at http://localhost:8082. The admin credentials are username 'admin' and password 'password'.
